title: UI-state browser QA — custom pages render + tolerate interaction (UI-001/UI-007/UI-010)
business_reason: P0.10 demands the UI be clickable, usable and real. Prove the custom staff pages actually render in a browser (no blank screen, no runtime errors) and tolerate rapid interaction.

design.tests: tests/e2e/ui_states.spec.ts (Playwright browser): for each custom page (security-audit/integrity, reports/exports, admin/settings/changes) the heading is visible (UI-001 no blank screen) with zero uncaught pageerrors (UI-010); plus a double-click on the verify button (UI-007) raises no runtime error.
design.scope: Visual/mobile/empty-state/a11y cases (UI-002/005/011/012) remain manual or visual-regression QA, documented in NEGATIVE_TEST_STATUS.md (not reliably automatable in the server harness).

param_14.question: Do the staff pages actually render and work in a browser?
param_14.answer: Yes — a browser test loads each custom staff page, asserts the heading renders (no blank screen) with no uncaught runtime errors, and that an interactive control tolerates a double-click. Visual/mobile/accessibility checks remain a tracked manual/visual-regression QA pass.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: viewport/mobile + axe a11y + empty-state assertions; visual-regression baseline.
